Her, i lommen min, tror jeg det er en gammel Barlow-kniv som jeg har båret i flere år. Nå, jeg ser ikke den kniven. Jeg føler ikke den kniven. Jeg smaker ikke den kniven. Jeg lukter ikke den kniven. Men jeg vet at den kniven er der. Hvordan vet jeg det? Fordi jeg tror den er der. Nå, hvis den ikke er der, vil troen bringe den dit, hvis den ikke er der.
Det er vanskelig å forstå, jeg vet. Men det er sant. Hvis du tror, er alt mulig. Du er redd for å stole på Gud, venner. Du er skremt. Nå, jeg kan ha latt den kniven ligge på kommoden der jeg skiftet klær for en stund siden. Men like sikkert som den kniven er på kommoden nå, hvis jeg tror på det av hele mitt hjerte, vil den være i lommen min. Det virker uforståelig, men Gud er i stand til å sette den der hvis du tror det.

text:
Here, I want to show you something. Come here, Reverend Kidson. Is this Sister Morse? Would you stand up just a moment?
I want to show you something, friends. Just stand right here. And you don't mind, that's not making a--a public show for you, sister, but it's for the glory of God.
Now, look friends, I want to show you something. I believe there's a--a woman standing in front of me. Could you tell me I'm wrong? [Brother Kidson replies, "You don't…"] But… [You don't hear any woman?"] No, I don't hear any woman. ["You don't feel any woman?"] No, sir. Don't know how I know there's one there? ["You don't taste any woman?"] No. ["How can you tell there's a woman there?"] Because the sense of sight tells me I see a woman. ["Hum, well, are you sure?"] I'm positive there's a woman standing there. Do you believe that I'm right? ["Yes."] Audience, you think I am right? Now, is it possible I could be wrong? Yes. Every one of us may be blinded of our eyes. That might be a vision. Remember the five sense of the body is not so direct. Faith is positive.
text_no:
Kom hit, Reverend Kidson. Er dette Søster Morse? Kan du reise deg et øyeblikk?
Jeg vil vise dere noe, venner. Bare stå her. Dette er ikke for å lage et offentlig show for deg, Søster, men for Guds ære.
Se her, venner. Jeg tror det står en kvinne foran meg. Kan du si at jeg tar feil? [Bror Kidson svarer: "Du gjør ikke…"] Men... ["Hører du ingen kvinne?"] Nei, jeg hører ingen kvinne. ["Føler du ingen kvinne?"] Nei, sir. Hvordan vet jeg at det er en der? ["Smaker du noen kvinne?"] Nei. ["Hvordan kan du si at det står en kvinne der?"] Fordi synssansen forteller meg at jeg ser en kvinne. ["Er du sikker?"] Jeg er positiv at det står en kvinne der. Tror dere jeg har rett? ["Ja."] Publikum, tror dere jeg har rett? 
Er det mulig at jeg tar feil? Ja. Vi kan alle bli blinde for våre egne øyne. Dette kan være en visjon. Husk, de fem sansene våre er ikke alltid direkte. Tro er positiv.

text: Now, I know there's a woman standing there though, because that the sense of sight tells me there's a woman standing there. You see what I mean? Now, you think you could argue me down, tell me there's no woman standing there. Then you try it one time. You try to tell me I'm not looking at a woman standing before me. You think you could out argue me? No, sir, because I know this sense of sight is direct, and I'm looking at that woman, and I know she's standing there. Yet the only thing that I know is the sense of sight. Now, I want you to notice. Now, I'm going to close my eyes. Now, I feeled a woman's hand. [End of record]
text_no: Jeg vet at det står en kvinne der, fordi synssansen forteller meg at det står en kvinne der. Skjønner du hva jeg mener? Tror du at du kunne argumentert mot meg og sagt at det ikke står en kvinne der? Prøv det en gang. Prøv å fortelle meg at jeg ikke ser en kvinne foran meg. Tror du at du kunne overgått meg i en slik diskusjon? Nei, for jeg vet at synssansen er direkte, og jeg ser på den kvinnen og vet at hun står der. Synssansen er den eneste måten jeg vet det på. Nå, følg med. Jeg lukker øynene. Nå føler jeg en kvinnes hånd. [Slutt på opptaket]

text:
I can tell it's a woman. She has a woman's hat on. She has a woman's hair; it's a woman's hand. I know it's a woman, because I feel her. You think you could out argue me? ["No."] Huh? That's directly; I know it, that that was a woman. I've got my back turned to her, but yet I know it's a woman. See? Because the sense of feeling tells me there's a woman there. You see what I mean?
Now, sight told me there's one there now. I don't feel her. That's the sense of sight. I know she's standing there, because I'm looking at her. I close my eyes. I know she's standing there now, because--not because I see her; because I feel her hand. I know she's standing there. I don't see her, but yet I feel her. The sense of feeling declares there's a woman standing there. See what I mean?
text_no:
Jeg kan si at det er en kvinne. Hun har en kvinnes hatt på. Hun har en kvinnes hår; det er en kvinnes hånd. Jeg vet det er en kvinne, fordi jeg føler det. Tror du at du kunne vinne en diskusjon mot meg? [«Nei.»] Huh? Det er direkte; jeg vet at det er en kvinne. Jeg har ryggen til henne, men likevel vet jeg at det er en kvinne. Ser du? Fordi følelsen forteller meg at det er en kvinne der. Skjønner du hva jeg mener?
Nå, synet forteller meg at det er noen der nå. Jeg føler henne ikke. Det er synssansen. Jeg vet hun står der, fordi jeg ser på henne. Jeg lukker øynene. Jeg vet hun står der nå, ikke fordi jeg ser henne, men fordi jeg føler hånden hennes. Jeg vet hun står der. Jeg ser henne ikke, men likevel føler jeg henne. Følesansen forteller meg at en kvinne står der. Skjønner du hva jeg mener?

text:
Now, faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence (See?), direct positive, just the same as my sight is positive, same as my feeling's positive… It's the evidence of things not seen, just as positive as that is. You see what I mean?
So many people try to work theirself up and say, "Oh, I--I got faith." But, friends, it's not there. If it was, it would react. See? You know what I mean?
text_no:
Tro er substansen av ting som håpes på, beviset (se?), direkte positivt, på samme måte som synet mitt er positivt, samme som følelsene mine er positive… Det er beviset på ting som ikke sees, like positivt som det. Forstår du hva jeg mener?
Mange mennesker prøver å arbeide seg opp og si: "Å, jeg--jeg har tro." Men, venner, det er ikke der. Hvis det var, ville det reagere. Ser du? Forstår du hva jeg mener?

text:
Now, look… Thank you, Sister Morse and God bless you now.
Now, look. Now, I seen the woman; my sense of sight said she was there. Then I knew she was there by the sense of sight. I closed my eyes; I didn't have sight. Then I felt her, and the sense of feeling said she was there. The sense of feeling was just as direct as the sense of sight, because it was a direct evidence.
Now, faith is a direct evidence of things not seen, felt, taste, smelled, or heard. Now, look here. I'm going to close my eyes now. Watch.
text_no:
Takk, Søster Morse, og Gud velsigne deg.
Nå, se her. Jeg så kvinnen; mitt synssans fortalte meg at hun var der. Derfor visste jeg at hun var der på grunn av synssansen. Jeg lukket øynene, og hadde ikke lenger syn. Så følte jeg henne, og følelsessansen sa at hun var der. Følelsessansen var like direkte som synssansen, fordi det var en direkte bevis.
Tro er et direkte bevis på ting som ikke kan sees, føles, smakes, luktes eller høres. Se her. Jeg skal lukke øynene nå. Følg med.

text:
That was orange juice. [Brother Kidson says, "You don't see any oranges."] No, sir. ["You don't hear any oranges."] No, sir. ["You don't feel any oranges."] No, sir. ["You don't smell any oranges."] No, sir. ["What makes you think that is orange?"] Because I tasted it. ["Are you positive?"] I'm positive that was orange juice. That would not have been lemon; it would not have been grape. It would not have been grapefruit. That was orange juice, because the sense of taste. I--I didn't smell it; I didn't hear it; I didn't feel it; I didn't see it; but yet the sense of taste told me that was orange juice. You think I'm right? ["You're right."]
Sure it is. Why, the sense of taste proved that it was orange juice. Now, no matter how much you tell me it was water; I know it's orange juice. I tasted it. See? You could taste it too. And if you've got a sense of taste, you would know it the same. And if you've got faith you'll know the same thing I'm talking about. Now, watch here again, now watch here.
I smell perfume. That's perfume. [Brother Kidson says, "You can't see any."] I don't see any perfume. ["You don't hear any."] I don't hear any perfume. ["You don't taste any."] I don't taste it. ["You don't feel any."] I don't feel it. ["What makes you think that's perfume?"] 'Cause I smell it. ["Amen."] That's right. The sense of smell tells me that was perfume before my nose. [Scratch on the record]… me? Try it. All right, know that was perfume. I didn't see it, did I? Did I see it? I had my eyes closed. Did I taste it? No, I didn't have it in my mouth. Did I feel it? Never had my hands about it, nor nothing else. How did I know it then? Did I hear it? No, I smelled it, and it was a direct evidence that that was perfume. I know it. [End of record.]
text_no:
Det var appelsinjuice. [Bror Kidson sier: "Du ser ingen appelsiner."] Nei, sir. ["Du hører ingen appelsiner."] Nei, sir. ["Du føler ingen appelsiner."] Nei, sir. ["Du lukter ingen appelsiner."] Nei, sir. ["Hva får deg til å tro at det er appelsin?"] Fordi jeg smakte det. ["Er du sikker?"] Jeg er sikker på at det var appelsinjuice. Det kunne ikke vært sitron; det kunne ikke vært drue. Det kunne ikke vært grapefrukt. Det var appelsinjuice, på grunn av smaken. Jeg luktet det ikke; jeg hørte det ikke; jeg følte det ikke; jeg så det ikke; men smaken fortalte meg at det var appelsinjuice. Mener du jeg har rett? ["Du har rett."]
Selvfølgelig. Smakssansen beviste at det var appelsinjuice. Uansett hvor mye du forteller meg at det var vann, vet jeg at det var appelsinjuice. Jeg smakte det. Ser du? Du kunne også smake det. Og om du har smakssans, ville du vite det samme. Og om du har tro, vil du vite det samme jeg snakker om. Nå, se her igjen, se her.
Jeg lukter parfyme. Det er parfyme. [Bror Kidson sier: "Du ser ingen."] Jeg ser ingen parfyme. ["Du hører ingen."] Jeg hører ingen parfyme. ["Du smaker ingen."] Jeg smaker det ikke. ["Du føler ingen."] Jeg føler det ikke. ["Hva får deg til å tro at det er parfyme?"] Fordi jeg lukter det. ["Amen."] Det er riktig. Luktesansen sier meg at det var parfyme foran nesen min. [Ripe på platen] ... meg? Prøv det. Greit, jeg vet at det var parfyme. Jeg så det ikke, gjorde jeg? Så jeg det? Jeg hadde øynene lukket. Smakte jeg det? Nei, jeg hadde det ikke i munnen. Følte jeg det? Hadde ikke hendene på det, eller noe annet. Hvordan visste jeg det? Hørte jeg det? Nei, jeg luktet det, og det var et klart bevis på at det var parfyme. Jeg vet det. [Slutt på platen.]

text: Now… [Brother Kidson says, "…don't feel any music."] No, sir. ["You don't taste any music."] No, sir. ["Well, what makes you think that's music?"] I hear it, and I know it's music. ["Are you sure about that?"] I'm positive. The lady's playing "Only Believe." You think I'm right? ["You're right."] Try to argue me out of it. I don't see it. I don't taste it. I don't feel it. I don't smell it. But I hear it. Was I positive? Do I know that I'm right? Why?
text_no: Nå... [Bror Kidson sier, "…kjenner du ikke noe musikk?"] Nei. ["Du smaker ikke musikken?"] Nei. ["Hva gjør at du tror det er musikk?"] Jeg hører det, og jeg vet det er musikk. ["Er du sikker på det?"] Jeg er helt sikker. Damen spiller "Only Believe." Tror du jeg har rett? ["Du har rett."] Prøv å motbevise meg. Jeg ser det ikke. Jeg smaker det ikke. Jeg føler det ikke. Jeg lukter det ikke. Men jeg hører det. Var jeg sikker? Vet jeg at jeg har rett? Hvorfor?

text:
Now, there is the five senses of the body. Now, they're positive, aren't they? You know when they happen. You know. Now, you're looking at a audience. You know you're looking at me.
Well, then friends, faith… Here it is. Faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen. It's positive. You know it. There's no need of anybody telling you anything more about it. It's just as much declared as any of these other five senses of the body. You know what I mean? How many has a general understanding now of what I mean? Let's see your hands. See, see?
text_no:
Vi har fem sanser i kroppen, og de er positive, er de ikke? Du vet når de aktiveres. Du vet at du ser på publikum, og du vet at du ser på meg. 
Vel, venner, tro… Her er tingen: Tro er et bevis på de tingene man håper på, og en overbevisning om de tingene man ikke ser. Det er positivt. Du vet det. Ingen trenger å fortelle deg mer om det. Tro er like tydelig som noen av de andre fem sansene i kroppen. Forstår dere hva jeg mener? Hvor mange av dere har en generell forståelse nå av hva jeg mener? La meg se hendene deres. Ser dere?

text:
It ain't what you try to make yourself believe. Now, for instance, I try to make myself believe I'm hearing music. I--I wouldn't do it. May I make myself believe I'm smelling perfume. It's just not there. I've got to absolutely do it. You see what I mean?
And faith is that positive. It is an act of positive. You know it. Oh, my, I hope you see it, what I mean.
text_no:
Det handler ikke om hva du prøver å få deg selv til å tro. For eksempel prøver jeg å få meg selv til å tro at jeg hører musikk, men jeg klarer det ikke. Eller jeg prøver å få meg selv til å tro at jeg dufter parfyme, men det er bare ikke der. Jeg må virkelig gjøre det. Forstår du hva jeg mener?
Tro er like definitivt. Det er en positiv handling. Du vet det. Å, jeg håper du forstår hva jeg mener.

text:
See, it's the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen. You don't see it. You don't taste it. Somebody say, "I don't feel any better."
Well, what's the matter? Why don't you feel any better? If you have your faith, according to your faith be it unto you. Is that right? See?
No matter how you feel, you're healed anyhow. No matter what you see, you're healed anyhow. See what I mean? It is a… You know it. There's nothing can take it out of your heart. You know it. It's just positive act right there. Oh, my.
If you could only see it, friends, something would take place. If you could only know what I'm talking about, something would react right now. See? It's a positive. It's right there, just as positive as these other five senses. These are of the natural man. Faith is of God. The natural man can be wrong, but God cannot be wrong. Oh, my.
text_no:
Se, det er substansen av ting man håper på, beviset på ting man ikke ser. Du ser det ikke. Du smaker det ikke. Noen sier, "Jeg føler meg ikke noe bedre." Vel, hva er i veien? Hvorfor føler du deg ikke noe bedre? Hvis du har din tro, vær det ifølge din tro. Er det riktig? Ser du?
Uansett hvordan du føler deg, er du helbredet uansett. Uansett hva du ser, er du helbredet uansett. Forstår du hva jeg mener? Det er en... Du vet det. Ingenting kan ta det ut av hjertet ditt. Du vet det. Det er bare en positiv handling der. Å, min.
Hvis du bare kunne se det, venner, ville noe skje. Hvis du bare kunne forstå hva jeg snakker om, ville noe reagere akkurat nå. Ser du? Det er positivt. Det er der, like sikkert som disse andre fem sansene. Disse tilhører det naturlige mennesket. Tro er fra Gud. Det naturlige mennesket kan ta feil, men Gud kan ikke ta feil. Å, min.

text: Why, it would make us go to shouting. Think of it. And people tell me they got faith and say they can't believe in Divine healing? Friends, if you don't believe in Divine healings, you're lost. That's right. How you going to, if you can't have faith enough for God to patch up this body to glorify Him in, how much more you going to have faith to believe that God will take this old mortal and make immortality out of it to take it up. That's a direct Divine healing. Oh, my, there's going to be some awful disappointments at the day of judgment, at the resurrection. That's right.
text_no: Tenk på det. Noen sier de har tro og hevder samtidig at de ikke kan tro på guddommelig helbredelse? Venner, hvis dere ikke tror på guddommelig helbredelse, er dere fortapt. Det er sant. Hvordan skal dere ha nok tro til å tro at Gud vil forvandle denne dødelige kroppen til en udødelig en som vil bli tatt opp, hvis dere ikke engang har nok tro til å tro at Gud kan helbrede denne kroppen for å ære Ham? Det er en direkte guddommelig helbredelse. Åh, det kommer til å bli noen fryktelige skuffelser på dommens dag, ved oppstandelsen. Det er sant.

text: Faith… We've got to get in such a place till we be translated like Enoch was. That's right. Faith, testimony, that it pleased God. Did not Enoch… Our following Scripture said that Enoch had a testimony by faith that he pleased God. Is that right? And the Bible said, "Without faith it's impossible to please God." So you've got to have the same kind of faith that you believe, and know. [End of record.]
text_no: Tro... Vi må komme til et sted der vi blir forvandlet slik Enok ble. Det stemmer. Tro, vitnesbyrdet om at det behaget Gud. Hadde ikke Enok... Skriften sier at Enok hadde et vitnesbyrd ved tro om at det behaget Gud. Er det riktig? Og Bibelen sier: "Uten tro er det umulig å behage Gud." Så du må ha den samme typen tro slik at du tror og vet. [Slutt på opptaket.]

text: A gift of healing, not a gift of miracles, though many miracles are performed right along with it. But it's a gift of healing; the people get well. They go out, prayed for, they come back. And my, they're healed. Wait till I leave the city, friends. After I'm gone a long time you'll hear the people coming to your churches and saying, "Well, that cancer, it's gone." "That--that deafness I had, why, I--I hear now." "My affliction, why, it just left me."
text_no: En gave til helbredelse, ikke en gave til mirakler, selv om mange mirakler utføres sammen med den. Men det er en gave til helbredelse; folk blir friske. De blir bedt for og kommer tilbake helbredet. Bare vent til jeg forlater byen, venner. Etter at jeg har vært borte en stund, vil dere høre folk komme til deres menigheter og si: "Vel, den kreften er borte." "Den døvheten jeg hadde, hvorfor, jeg hører nå." "Min lidelse, hvorfor, den forlot meg bare."

text:
It's just the simplicity of… It's nothing you can work yourself up to. It's no excitement. I notice people come in line, just get to shaking themselves, and saying, "Oh, God, hallelujah. Amen. Glory to God."
Don't do that. You interfere with faith there. You interfere with God. Come up reverently and just lay your hand up and believe. That's all. Faith, you don't work yourself up to it. Faith is something that's already in your heart. You just come and believe. You see what I mean?
Oh, friends, my, I love you. I wouldn't talk to you so straight if I didn't love you. But I love you too well to see you get by without this. You see? You--you must understand.
text_no:
Det er bare enkelheten i det... Det er ikke noe du kan jobbe deg opp til. Det er ingen spenning. Jeg merker at folk kommer i køen og begynner å riste på seg selv, og sier, "Å Gud, halleluja. Amen. Ære være Gud."
Ikke gjør det. Du forstyrrer troen der. Du forstyrrer Gud. Kom ærbødig frem og bare legg hånden din på og tro. Det er alt. Tro, du jobber deg ikke opp til det. Tro er noe som allerede er i hjertet ditt. Du bare kommer og tror. Skjønner du hva jeg mener?
Å, venner, jeg elsker dere. Jeg ville ikke snakke så direkte til dere hvis jeg ikke elsket dere. Men jeg elsker dere for mye til å se dere gå glipp av dette. Forstår dere?

text:
And now look, now to my text right quick so we can hurry up. God testified of Abel's gifts. God always testifies of His gifts. Is that right?
Now, I want you to notice, when the children of Israel were in bondage four hundred and twenty years down in Egypt, carried down there by Joseph when they went down… By and by there raised up a Jo--or a Pharaoh who did not know Joseph. And then the people got in bondage. And they had to make straw houses and bricks and things out of straw and stubbles. And then they begin to cry to God for a deliverer. Is that right? And they cried and cried. And God foreordained a deliverer (Is that right?), Moses.
text_no:
La oss raskt se på teksten slik at vi kan komme videre. Gud vitnet om Abels gaver. Gud vitner alltid om Sine gaver. Er det riktig?
Så, jeg vil at dere skal legge merke til når Israels barn var i trelldom i Egypt i fire hundre og tjue år, brakt dit av Josef da de dro ned... Etter en tid, kom en farao som ikke kjente Josef. Da kom folket i trelldom. De måtte lage hus og murstein av halm og stubb. Så begynte de å rope til Gud etter en befrier. Er det riktig? De ropte og ropte. Og Gud hadde forutbestemt en befrier, Moses. Er det riktig?

text:
And when Moses came to the age of maturity, he went out and seen two Egyptians mistreating a Hebrew, and he slew the Egyptians and buried them. And the next day when he met the Hebrews striving among one another, "Why," they said, "will you kill us as you did the Egyptian?"
Now, look, be quiet people. Moses, the very deliverer, God's gift to the people, they did not understand him. You see what I mean? They did not understand Moses. And Moses thought, surely, that they would understand that he was their gift to deliver them. But they did not understand.
And now, friends, don't get me wrong. May I say this with reverence in my heart, knowing that I'm an eternity bound person that'll stand before judgment someday.
Thousands of people are missing their gift. See? They can't understand it. And they'll look and say, "Oh, he's just a man."
That's true. Was it God or Moses that delivered the people? It was God in Moses. See? They cried for the deliverer. And when God sent the deliverer to them, they failed to see it, because it was by a man. But it wasn't a man; it was God in the man. [End of record]
text_no:
Da Moses nådde moden alder, gikk han ut og så to egyptere som mishandlet en hebreer. Han drepte dem og begravde dem. Dagen etter møtte han hebreere som kranglet med hverandre, og de spurte: "Hvorfor? Vil du drepe oss som du gjorde med egypteren?"
Vær oppmerksom, kjære folk. Moses, selve befrieren, Guds gave til folket, ble ikke forstått av dem. Ser dere hva jeg mener? De forsto ikke Moses. Moses tenkte at de selvfølgelig ville forstå at han var deres gave for å befri dem. Men de gjorde det ikke.
Nå, venner, misforstå meg ikke. Må jeg si dette med ærbødighet i hjertet, vitende om at jeg er en evighetsbundet person som en dag skal stå for dommen. 
Tusenvis av mennesker går glipp av sin gave. Ser dere? De kan ikke forstå det. De vil se og si: "Å, han er bare en mann."
Det er riktig. Var det Gud eller Moses som befridde folket? Det var Gud i Moses. Ser dere? De ropte etter en befrier. Og når Gud sendte befrieren til dem, klarte de ikke å se det, fordi det kom gjennom en mann. Men det var ikke mannen; det var Gud i mannen. [Slutt på opptaket]

text:
"… that dwelleth in Me. He's the One that doeth the works." You see? But He was God's Gift to the world. God gave Him for you and I. Aren't you happy about it? Look, God gave Him.
Well now, the people that believed that God give Him, they can be saved. But those who do not believe it, they can't be saved. Is that right?
And there's no other way at all to enter the Kingdom, only by Him. Is that right? Well now, if you believe it, you can be saved. But if you don't believe it, you can't be saved, because He is God's Gift.
Look, the Scriptures say, "No man… We know that God was with Christ, for no man could do the works that He done except God be with Him." Is that right?
Then what was it? God was testifying that that was His only begotten Son. Is that right? God testified that; He said: "This is My Son." He said, "If you don't believe Me, believe for the very work's sake." Is that right? He said, "The man testifies of himself," you know how the Scriptures read, "but if--if he testify and that what he says is not true, then believe Me not. But if I testify and that what I say is true, My Father will testify of it."
And if I tell you about a gift of Divine healing and God doesn't testify of it, leave it go; it's wrong. But if God testifies of it, believe it's God's gift to you. That's right. You see what I mean?
text_no:
"… som bor i Meg. Han er den som utfører verkene." Ser du? Men Han var Guds gave til verden. Gud ga Ham for deg og meg. Er du ikke glad for det? Se, Gud ga Ham.
Nå, de som tror at Gud ga Ham, kan bli frelst. Men de som ikke tror det, kan ikke bli frelst. Er det riktig? 
Det er ingen annen måte å komme inn i Guds Rike på enn gjennom Ham. Er det riktig? Nå, hvis du tror det, kan du bli frelst. Men hvis du ikke tror det, kan du ikke bli frelst, fordi Han er Guds gave.
Se, Skriften sier, "Ingen… Vi vet at Gud var med Kristus, for ingen kunne gjøre de verkene Han gjorde uten at Gud var med Ham." Er det riktig? 
Så, hva var det? Gud vitnet om at dette var Hans enbårne Sønn. Er det riktig? Gud vitnet om det; Han sa: "Dette er Min Sønn." Han sa, "Hvis dere ikke tror Meg, tro for selve verkets skyld." Er det riktig? Han sa, "Mennesket vitner om seg selv," du vet hvordan Skriften lyder, "men hvis—hvis han vitner og det han sier ikke er sant, så tro Meg ikke. Men hvis Jeg vitner og det Jeg sier er sant, vil Min Far vitne om det."
Og hvis Jeg forteller dere om en gave av guddommelig helbredelse og Gud ikke vitner om det, la det være; det er galt. Men hvis Gud vitner om det, tro at det er Guds gave til dere. Det er riktig. Forstår du hva jeg mener?

text:
I hate to have to bring this out like this, friends, but I'm trusting you're getting between the lines of it. Now, notice. See, it's God's gift. After Jesus, when He went away… He was taken up. Many people came to Jesus that never got healed. He prayed for many who never had no results of it, because they didn't believe. They didn't believe that He was God's Son. They said, "Why, He makes Hisself God. He forgives sins on earth: blasphemy." Don't you see?
They said it about Elisha. They said the same thing about Moses. "Why, we would that we had died down in Egypt, than be brought out here to starve in the wilderness."
See, God's messenger has always been rejected. You know what I'm talking of, don't you? I hope you do, friends. God bless your hearts. I--I want you to see it, friends.
text_no:
Jeg misliker å måtte ta opp dette på denne måten, venner, men jeg stoler på at dere forstår hva som ligger mellom linjene. Legg merke til dette. Det er Guds gave. Etter at Jesus ble tatt opp til Himmelen, var det mange som kom til Ham uten å bli helbredet. Han ba for mange som ikke opplevde resultater, fordi de ikke trodde. De trodde ikke at Han var Guds Sønn. De sa: "Hvorfor gjør Han Seg Selv til Gud? Han tilgir synder her på jorden: blasfemi." Ser dere ikke?
De sa det samme om Elisha. De sa det samme om Moses. "Hvorfor, vi skulle ønske vi hadde dødd i Egypt, i stedet for å bli ført hit for å sulte i ørkenen."
Guds sendebud har alltid blitt avvist. Dere vet hva jeg snakker om, ikke sant? Jeg håper det, venner. Gud velsigne dere. Jeg vil at dere skal se det, venner.

text:
Now, then they were in need of a Saviour. God sent Christ to the world for a Saviour.
Then after Jesus going away, many of the beloved people of God were sick and afflicted. God gave Peter the gift of Divine healing. Now, it… the people got healed because God testified through Peter of the gift of Divine healing just like he testified through Elisha as a prophet, through Moses as a deliverer, through Christ as a Son. He testified through Peter as a healer. Did he do it? And the people got the idea. And they even got such a mob of people, that Peter couldn't pray for each one of them, and they knew that this apostle was God's gift to them. And he never shook their hands and said, "God bless you." They took the people on the outside of the building and laid them down on the street that even his shadow would pass over them and they would be healed. Is that right? Why? Because they knew that Peter was the gift of healing sent from God to the people. Oh, I hope you see it, friends, look. You see, it was God's gift. What now, I want to ask you, was it Peter done the healing? [End of record]
text_no: Da trengte de en Frelser. Gud sendte Kristus til verden som en Frelser. Etter at Jesus dro bort, var mange av Guds elskede folk syke og plaget. Gud ga Peter gaven av guddommelig helbredelse. Folk ble helbredet fordi Gud vitnet gjennom Peter om gaven av guddommelig helbredelse, akkurat som Han vitnet gjennom Elisja som profet, gjennom Moses som frigjører og gjennom Kristus som Sønn. Han vitnet gjennom Peter som helbreder. Gjorde Han det? Folk fikk forståelsen av dette. Det var så mange mennesker at Peter ikke kunne be for hver enkelt av dem. De visste at denne apostelen var Guds gave til dem. Peter trengte ikke å håndhilse og si "Gud velsigne deg." De førte folk utenfor bygningen og la dem ned på gaten slik at til og med skyggen hans kunne passere over dem og helbrede dem. Er det riktig? Hvorfor? Fordi de visste at Peter var helbredelsens gave sendt fra Gud til folket. Åh, jeg håper dere ser det, venner. Se. Dere ser, det var Guds gave. Nå vil jeg spørre dere, var det Peter som gjorde helbredelsen? [Slutt på opptaket]

text:
God is not in material things. God is in men. The Holy Ghost never fell on organizations; it fell on men. And so many people today say, "They don't belong to my faith." Well, if you're the faith of Christ, it's all the same. God doesn't care about… Your organization don't mean that to Him. And the peoples got theirself to a place it's just a big organized world. And it--that's the reason I'd rather have it in a auditorium.
If it's in a--a Oneness church, the Trinity won't come. If it's a Trinity's church, the Oneness won't come. If it's in a Methodist church, the Baptists won't come. If it's in a Baptist church, the Methodists won't come. One body… One person said, "Should we be baptized over now in order to come, be prayed for?"
Mercy, that's as pagan as we ever had it. That's right. No. For by one Spirit we're all baptized into one body (That's right.), regardless of who you are and where you are. That's right. So it don't make any difference what organization you are. You don't have to belong to the Baptist church, or the Methodist church, or the Pentecost, or whatever it is. You have to have faith in God. That's right.
Now, and frankly, you don't have to belong to no church. But if you get healed and go sin again, it'll come back on you again worse than it was in the first place. You go somewhere then to church and serve God. God don't heal you for the devil's glory. He heals you for His glory.
text_no:
Gud finnes ikke i materielle ting, Han er i mennesker. Den Hellige Ånd falt aldri på organisasjoner; Den falt på mennesker. Mange sier i dag: "De tilhører ikke min tro." Vel, hvis du er i Kristi tro, er det det samme. Gud bryr Seg ikke om organisasjoner. Deres organisasjon betyr ikke noe for Ham. Folk har organisert seg så mye at verden er blitt en stor organisasjon. Derfor foretrekker jeg å ha det i et auditorium.
Hvis det er i en Oneness-menighet, kommer ikke Trinitarerne. Hvis det er i en trinitarisk menighet, kommer ikke Oneness. Hvis det er i en metodistmenighet, kommer ikke baptister. Hvis det er i en baptistmenighet, kommer ikke metodister. En person spurte: "Må vi bli døpt på nytt for å bli bedt for?"
Dette er så hedensk som vi noen gang har hatt det. Nei, for ved én Ånd er vi alle døpt inn i ett legeme, uavhengig av hvem du er og hvor du er. Det spiller ingen rolle hvilken konfesjon du tilhører. Du trenger ikke å tilhøre den baptistiske, metodistiske eller pinsemenigheten. Du må ha tro på Gud.
Faktisk trenger du ikke å tilhøre noen menighet. Men hvis du blir helbredet og synder igjen, vil sykdommen komme tilbake, verre enn før. Du bør da gå til en menighet og tjene Gud. Gud helbreder deg ikke til djevelens ære. Han helbreder deg til Sin egen ære.

text:
Now, look: how the people fail. Now, Moses felt, I'd imagine how he felt. He just hated to come right out and tell the people, because he thought it'd be self-exaltation. He thought the people'd say, "Now, look at that man."
We've had a lot of that in the world today. We've had a lot of people come through said, "God done this and He done that." But God didn't bear record of it. But if God bears record, He will respect His gift. Is that right?
text_no:
Se nå: hvordan folkene faller. Jeg kan forestille meg hvordan Moses følte det. Han mislikte å fortelle det rett ut til folket, fordi han fryktet det ville oppfattes som selvhevdelse. Han trodde folk ville si: "Se på den mannen."
Vi har sett mye av dette i verden i dag. Mange har kommet og sagt, "Gud gjorde dette og Han gjorde det." Men Gud ga ikke sitt vitnesbyrd om det. Men hvis Gud gir sitt vitnesbyrd, vil Han respektere Sin gave. Er det ikke riktig?

text:
Yes, sir, God honored the gift of--of Abel. He honored the gift of Moses. And Moses thought the people would understand. But they did not understand.
And today, surely, looks like a person wouldn't have to come down any closer than what you are right now to know what I'm speaking of. All right, but it goes over the majority's head. And, friends, I love you, and I want to see you get well.
Now, when they called for a deliverer, God sent the deliverer, and they failed to see it. Moses was God's gift to Israel. You believe it?
text_no:
Ja, Gud æret Abels gave. Han æret også Moses' gave. Og Moses trodde folk ville forstå, men de gjorde det ikke.
I dag ser det ut som om man ikke skulle behøve å komme noe nærmere enn dere er nå for å forstå hva jeg snakker om. Likevel går det over hodet på de fleste. Venner, jeg elsker dere og ønsker å se dere bli friske.
Da de ba om en frelser, sendte Gud en frelser, men de skjønte det ikke. Moses var Guds gave til Israel. Tror dere det?

text:
On down, Elijah the prophet, they were God's gift. Many of them raised up false prophets. Right in the days of Elijah there raised up false prophets, but God testified of the prophecy of Elijah. If it's of God, God will testify of it. And there was Elijah, and he was God's gift to Israel. You believe it?
He was even Ahab's pastor. He was Jezebel's pastor. She didn't want to admit that he was her pastor, but he was the man who told her where she was living. That's right. He was her pastor. She hated him, but yet he was God's provided gift for her down there to let her know her sins; and she failed to see him. You know what I'm speaking of?
text_no:
Elia, profeten, var en gave fra Gud. Mange falske profeter reiste seg i hans tid. Men Gud bevitnet Elias profetier. Hvis det er fra Gud, vil Han bekrefte det. Elia var Guds gave til Israel. Tror du det?
Han var til og med Ahab og Jesabels pastor. Selv om Jesabel ikke ville innrømme at han var hennes pastor, var han den som avslørte hennes synder. Hun hatet ham, men han var Guds gave til henne for å vise henne veien. Hun klarte ikke å se det. Forstår du hva jeg mener?

text:
Along come Jesus. And Jesus… Listen. Jesus was God's gift to Israel, to the world. Is that right? Jesus was God's gift. "God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son." You believe it? [End of record]
And it wasn't the apostles. Do you think Moses was the--the deliverer because it was Moses, his wits and his intelligence? Why, he said he was a man of slow speech. It was God in Moses, a gift to the people. It was God in Christ. He said, "It's not Me; it's My Father that dwelleth in Me."
That's the reason Martha said, "Lord…" (last night's sermon) Said, "I know that Thou art the Christ, the--the Son of God that should come into the world." She believed it. And there the people believed on Him.
text_no:
Så kom Jesus. Og Jesus... Hør her. Jesus var Guds gave til Israel og til verden. Er ikke det riktig? Jesus var Guds gave. "For så har Gud elsket verden at Han ga Sin enbårne Sønn." Tror du det? [Slutt på opptaket]
Og det var ikke apostlene. Tror du Moses var befrieren på grunn av sin egen kløkt og intelligens? Nei, han sa selv at han var treig til å tale. Det var Gud i Moses, en gave til folket. Det var Gud i Kristus. Han sa: "Det er ikke Meg; det er Min Far som bor i Meg."
Det er derfor Martha sa, "Herre..." (i gårsdagens preken) Hun sa: "Jeg vet at Du er Messias, Guds Sønn som skal komme til verden." Hun trodde det. Og der trodde folket på Ham.

text:
And look at Peter when he was walking by, why, he was a mortal. He was born of sinful flesh just like I or you. That's right. But the people had need of a healer, so they sent--God sent Peter to do that specific work. John was love. Peter didn't have the love in his heart that John had. John was God's agency of love. You see?
Look here, when He taken Peter, James, and John, hope, faith, and charity… Don't you see? Those gifted people, them three, the hope, faith, and charity…
Hope was in James; he was the one who set the church in order. Faith was in Peter. And charity was in John. Hope, faith, and charity, it was God's representatives on the earth.
And the people laid in Peter's shadow and were healed. Oh, friends. Oh, my. Don't think I'm yelling at you, friends. You might think I'm excited, but I'm not. I'm trying to get my point to you close enough so you'll see what I'm talking about.
text_no:
Se på Peter. Han var et menneske som oss, født i syndig kjøtt. Men folket trengte en helbreder, og Gud sendte Peter for å utføre det spesifikke arbeidet. Johannes representerte kjærligheten. Peter hadde ikke den kjærligheten som Johannes hadde. Johannes var Guds redskap for kjærlighet. 
Når Jesus tok med seg Peter, Jakob, og Johannes, representerte de håp, tro og kjærlighet. Ser dere? De tre, håp, tro og kjærlighet, var spesielle gaver.
Jakob, som satte menigheten i orden, representerte håp. Tro var i Peter. Og kjærlighet var i Johannes. Håp, tro og kjærlighet—Guds representanter på jorden.
Menneskene ble helbredet når de sto i Peters skygge. Å, venner! Ikke tro at jeg roper til dere. Dere kanskje tror jeg er oppspilt, men det er jeg ikke. Jeg prøver bare å få frem poenget mitt tydelig nok slik at dere ser hva jeg snakker om.

text:
Now, Pentecost people has been praying for the last thirty or forty years for a gift of healing.
We're living in the days when we've got the best medical science we ever had. We got the best hospitals we ever had. We got the best doctors we ever had. We got the best drugs we ever practiced with. And we got more sickness than we ever had.
There's thousands and hundreds of thousands of precious saints of God suffering tonight. And they are crying and crying and cried for years for God to restore the gift of Divine healing to the church. And when God moved the orbits thirty-seven years ago, and foreordained it and sent it, many hundreds of people are passing by without recognizing it, friends.
text_no:
Pinsefolk har bedt i de siste tretti til førti årene om en gave til helbredelse. Vi lever i en tid hvor vi har den beste medisinske vitenskapen som noen gang har eksistert. Vi har de beste sykehusene, de beste legene, og de beste medisinene vi noen gang har brukt. Likevel har vi mer sykdom enn noensinne.
Tusenvis, ja hundretusenvis, av Guds dyrebare hellige lider i natt. De har grått og ropt til Gud i årevis for å gjenopprette den guddommelige helbredelsesgaven til menigheten. Og da Gud beveget himmellegemene for trettisju år siden, forordnet og sendte gaven, har mange hundre mennesker gått forbi uten å anerkjenne den, venner.

text:
Now, it's brought to pass. I hate to say this. God have mercy. Look, friends, down through the rest of the country, over in Phoenix, over in Houston, over in San Antonio, down in Arkansas, down across the country, thousands are being healed, coming through there, of all kinds of sickness and diseases. Oakland, know your day. Someday, I shall meet you before the judgment bar of God, and you'll know I testified to you the God Almighty's truth. You cried for it. What? If you, just as you respect it as you believe it, God will testify of His gifts. That's right. He did for Abel. He did for Elijah. He did for Moses. He did for Peter. He did for Paul. He did for all, and He will do it yet today.
When Pentecost first fell and the baptism of the Holy Spirit first begin to fall, the people said, "It's Fanaticism." But God testified of it, that He was so. That's why I'm with you tonight, because I know it's the hand of God. Many of them went off into isms with it, but there's a genuine Pentecostal article. God has testified of it. That's true. And the same thing He's done tonight in another manner. [End of record.]
text_no:
Nå er det gått i oppfyllelse. Jeg hater å si dette. Gud ha nåde. Se, venner, rundt om i resten av landet, i Phoenix, i Houston, i San Antonio, nede i Arkansas, over hele landet, tusenvis blir helbredet fra alle slags sykdommer og plager. Oakland, kjenn din tid. En dag vil jeg møte dere foran Guds dommersete, og dere vil vite at jeg vitnet for dere om den Allmektige Guds sannhet. Dere ba om det. Hva så? Hvis dere respekterer og tror på det, vil Gud vitne om Sine gaver. Det er riktig. Han gjorde det for Abel. Han gjorde det for Elia. Han gjorde det for Moses. Han gjorde det for Peter. Han gjorde det for Paulus. Han gjorde det for alle, og Han vil gjøre det i dag.
Da pinsebevegelsen først begynte og Den Hellige Ånds dåp først kom, sa folk, "Det er fanatisme." Men Gud vitnet om at det var sant. Det er derfor jeg er med dere i kveld, fordi jeg vet at det er Guds hånd. Mange har gått over i sekter med det, men det finnes en ekte pinseartikkel. Gud har vitnet om det. Det er sant. Og Han har gjort det samme i kveld på en annen måte. [Slutt på opptaket.]

text:
And I was going into the drug store. And when I went in, I noticed some fellow looking at me kind of strangely. I went on in the store, and I come back out, and I had the bottle cap. And I never forgot about him. And I was still had my uniform on.
And he put his… The man put his hand on my shoulder, and he looked at me; he said, "Sir," said, "I seen you was an officer." Said, "I thought I'd ask you a question." Said, "You may think I'm kind of crazy."
And I said, "What's the matter, sir?"
He said, "I live in Paducah, Kentucky." He said, "I've been failing in health for about two years." He said, "Last week I had a peculiar dream." He said, "I dreamed that I should come to Jeffersonville, Indiana, and get somebody by the name of Branham to pray for me." Said, "Do you know whether there's any Branhams live here or not?"
My, you don't know how I felt. I thought, "Oh, God's testifying."
And I said, "Yes, sir." I said, "I know there's Branhams live here."
He said, "You know, is there anybody prays for the sick?"
I said, "I'm the one."
He just looked at me like that, and tears begin to roll down his cheeks. He just took me by the hand. I took him by the hand, took off my hat, laid my hat and gun down in my hat, knelt down on the side of the street and begin praying for him right there on the street.
When I raised up there was mothers standing, holding the little children back, and men with their hats off like that, waiting for the prayer.
There's been many things done in Jeffersonville, and they knew what was going on. And when we raised up, there they all were standing there with their heads down listening to the prayer.
I raised up. And the brother, he put his arms around me, commenced to patting me like that. I've never seen him from this day to that. God healed him.
text_no:
Jeg gikk inn på apoteket. Da jeg gikk inn, la jeg merke til en mann som så litt merkelig på meg. Jeg fortsatte inn i butikken, og da jeg kom tilbake ut, hadde jeg flaskehetten med meg. Jeg glemte aldri ham. Jeg hadde fortsatt på meg uniformen.
Mannen la hånden sin på skulderen min og så på meg; han sa, "Sir, jeg så at du var en offiser. Jeg tenkte å stille deg et spørsmål. Du synes kanskje jeg virker litt gal."
Jeg sa, "Hva er i veien, sir?"
Han sa, "Jeg bor i Paducah, Kentucky. Jeg har vært syk i omtrent to år." Han fortsatte, "Forrige uke hadde jeg en merkelig drøm. Jeg drømte at jeg skulle reise til Jeffersonville, Indiana, for å finne noen ved navn Branham som kunne be for meg. Vet du om det bor noen Branhams her?"
Å, du aner ikke hvordan jeg følte meg. Jeg tenkte, "Gud vitner."
Jeg sa, "Ja, sir. Jeg vet det bor Branhams her."
Han spurte, "Er det noen som ber for syke?"
Jeg svarte, "Det er meg."
Han så bare på meg, og tårene begynte å trille nedover kinnene hans. Han tok meg i hånden. Jeg tok av meg hatten, la hatten og pistolen ned på bakken, knelte på gaten og begynte å be for ham der og da.
Da jeg reiste meg, sto det mødre med barna sine og menn med hattene av, alle ventende på bønnen. Det har skjedd mye i Jeffersonville, og de visste hva som foregikk. Da vi reiste oss, sto de der med hodene bøyd og lyttet til bønnen.
Jeg reiste meg opp, og broren la armene rundt meg og begynte å klappe meg på ryggen. Jeg har ikke sett ham siden. Gud helbredet ham.

text:
And I was standing down at Houston, Texas, Reverend Kidson's meetings. And one morning standing there, oh, my, how I was thinking, praying. There was, we was having day service. And there was just hundreds of people coming in the line, being healed.
And all at once there was a lady that ran in. Oh, the building was about as long as this auditorium, main floor here. And the lady run into the back. She had a little boy in her arms, and she set down. And she was real restless. And the prayer line about four in abreast was coming by like this.
And she raised up; she said, "Just a minute, just a minute." And everyone stopped.
She said, "I don't like to stop that prayer line, but I want to tell you…"
She was real nervous and shaky, kind of excited.
text_no:
Jeg stod nede i Houston, Texas, på møtene til pastor Kidson. En morgen stod jeg der og tenkte og ba. Vi hadde dagsmøter, og det kom hundrevis av mennesker inn i køen for å bli helbredet.
Plutselig stormet en kvinne inn. Bygningen var omtrent like lang som denne hovedsalen her. Kvinnen løp inn bak, med en liten gutt i armene, og satte seg. Hun var veldig rastløs mens bønnekøen, med folk fire i bredden, beveget seg forbi.
Så reiste hun seg og ropte: "Vent litt, vent litt," og alle stoppet.
Hun sa: "Jeg vil ikke avbryte bønnekøen, men jeg må fortelle noe…"
Hun var veldig nervøs, skjelvende og litt oppspilt.

text:
She said, "I live over on the west side of Houston." Now, Houston's a pretty good size city, several miles across it, and we were on the east side. She said, "This morning my neighbor, the mother of this little child," said, "her father was an old Methodist preacher." Said, "He's been dead twenty years." And said, "Before he died, he used to go around preaching and saying someday God would give the gift back to the church for the Gentiles before the ending of the Gentile dispensation, like He did for the Jews."
And said, "His baby daughter, this is her baby." Said, "She's kind of weakly."
And said, "This morning after cleaning up her house, she'd set her dust mop up and was lying down across the foot of the bed and was resting." And said, "All at once she had a dream. She seen a big black cloud rising." And said, "Coming down out of that cloud, begin to move back." And said, "There come her dead father. And he said, 'Rise and take the child and go to the Houston Gospel Tabernacle. The gift of Divine healing is there.'"
Well, she just thought it was just a dream: never heard of the Houston Gospel Tabernacle. So she just laying there a little while and went to sleep again, and she dreamed it the second time. The big black cloud come. Her dead father come walking right down out of the cloud. She said, "Well, that's strange; I dreamed of dad the second time." Said, "He said, take the baby." Well, she got up and got her drink, stirred around the house a little bit and went back and lied--was lying down again. And when she went back, she dreamed it the third time. The big black cloud come. And her… [End of record]
text_no:
Hun sa: «Jeg bor på vestsiden av Houston.» Nå er Houston en ganske stor by, flere mil på tvers, og vi var på østsiden. Hun sa: «I morges fortalte naboen min, moren til dette lille barnet,» og sa videre, «hennes far var en gammel metodistforkynner.» Hun fortsatte: «Han har vært død i tjue år.» Og sa: «Før han døde, pleide han å reise rundt og forkynne og si at en dag ville Gud gi gaven tilbake til Menigheten for hedningene før avslutningen av hedningenes tidsalder, slik Han gjorde for jødene.»
Hun sa: «Hans datter, dette er hennes barn.» Hun la til: «Hun er litt svakelig.»
Hun sa: «Denne morgenen, etter å ha ryddet huset, hadde hun satt opp støvmoppen sin, lagt seg på sengen og hvilte.» Og sa: «Plutselig fikk hun en drøm. Hun så en stor svart sky stige opp.» Og sa: «Ut av den skyen kom hennes døde far gående. Han sa: 'Stå opp, ta barnet og gå til Houston Gospel Tabernacle. Gaven til guddommelig helbredelse er der.'»
Vel, hun trodde bare det var en drøm; hun hadde aldri hørt om Houston Gospel Tabernacle. Så hun ble liggende en stund til og sovnet igjen, og drømte det en gang til. Den store svarte skyen kom, og hennes døde far kom gående rett ut av skyen. Hun sa: «Det er rart; jeg drømte om pappa for andre gang.» Han sa: «Ta med barnet.» Vel, hun sto opp og hentet seg noe å drikke, ordnet litt i huset og la seg ned igjen. Da hun sovnet igjen, drømte hun det for tredje gang. Den store svarte skyen kom, og hennes... [Slutt på opptaket]

text:
They called the recorder's office to see if there was, just what was to the dream. I don't believe in all dreams now. So then, called, but he said, "Your young men shall see visions, your old men shall dream dreams."
And then she--she called the recorders office, and he said, "Yes, there's a Houston Gospel Tabernacle way over on the west side, on sixty-seventh street." And he said, "East sixty-seventh street."
And the lady, the mother, so weak she could not… Now, the child was--had infantile paralysis, his little legs was crippled. And she could not bring the child.
But she had the neighbor, and the neighbor kept changing busses and cars until she got over and got off of a car just about two blocks from the church. And there was an officer standing there. And she said to him, "Do you know where the Houston Gospel Tabernacle is?"
Said, "Yes, ma'am. Right up there where that crowd of people is standing there in the street," said, "They're having a healing service."
text_no:
De ringte til registerkontoret for å finne ut mer om drømmen. Jeg tror ikke på alle drømmer, men det står, "Deres unge menn skal se syner, deres gamle menn skal drømme drømmer."
Hun ringte til registerkontoret, og de sa, "Ja, det finnes en Houston Gospel Tabernacle på vestsiden, på seksti-sjuende gate." De sa deretter, "Øst seksti-sjuende gate."
Moren, som var så svak at hun ikke kunne... Barnet hadde spedbarnslammelse, og hans små bein var krøplige. Hun kunne ikke ta med barnet selv, men naboen hjalp til. Naboen byttet stadig busser og biler til hun kom fram, og gikk av omtrent to kvartaler fra menigheten. Der stod en politibetjent. Hun spurte ham, "Vet du hvor Houston Gospel Tabernacle er?"
Han svarte, "Ja, frue. Rett der borte hvor den folkemengden står i gaten. De holder en helbredelsestjeneste."

text:
Well, the poor thing was so weak when she got there, she, you imagine how she felt, walked right in there and seen that. And she told me. I said, "Bring me the baby."
Oh, my, you know what happened, don't you? Sure. And I thought, even though the people, the Angels of heaven know about it. God will testify of it. His works will be declared, regardless of what people think about it. That's right. It's true, friends. God bless your heart.
text_no:
Den stakkars kvinnen var så svak da hun kom dit. Du kan forestille deg hvordan hun følte seg da hun kom inn og så det. Hun fortalte meg det. Jeg sa, "Bring meg barnet."
Å, vet du hva som skjedde, ikke sant? Selvsagt. Jeg tenkte at selv om folk ikke vet det, så vet Himmelens Engler om det. Gud vil vitne om det. Hans gjerninger vil bli erklært, uansett hva folk mener om det. Det stemmer. Det er sant, venner. Gud velsigne hjertene deres.

text:
Here at Camden, Arkansas, with Brother Adams. I guess one of the Brother Adams' are here tonight? And at his place, he was pulling me out; we had thousands attend the service. And they were pulling me out of the service one day. And I kept hearing somebody hollering, "Have mercy, have mercy. Oh, where you at, Reverend?"
And I turned around, Brother Adams said, "You can't stop." It was a colored man, you know.
Yet even in Arkansas if you even shake the hands of a colored man they'll put you in jail. So there they are, such law they have there. You can talk to him, but don't put your hands on him.
Now, that's not right. That's not. He's a human just as good as I am, or you are, or anybody else. That's exactly right. I don't know what you think about that, but I want to unload my soul about it. That's right. Sure. I'm for Abraham Lincoln, and that's all right. And look…
text_no:
Her i Camden, Arkansas, sammen med Bror Adams. Jeg regner med at en av Bror Adams' er her i kveld? På hans sted dro han meg ut, vi hadde tusenvis som deltok på møtene. De dro meg ut av møtet en dag. Og jeg fortsatte å høre noen rope: "Ha barmhjertighet, ha barmhjertighet. Å, hvor er du, Pastor?"
Jeg snudde meg, og Bror Adams sa: "Du kan ikke stoppe." Det var en farget mann, vet du.
Til og med i Arkansas, hvis du håndhilser på en farget mann, kaster de deg i fengsel. Slik er lovene der. Du kan snakke med ham, men ikke ta på ham.
Dette er urett. Det er det. Han er like mye et menneske som jeg er, eller du er, eller noen andre. Det er helt riktig. Jeg vet ikke hva du mener om det, men jeg vil lette min sjel om det. Det er riktig. Selvsagt. Jeg støtter Abraham Lincoln, og det er greit. Og se…

text:
There this poor colored man standing there, hollering, "Oh Reverend."
And Brother Adams said, "Come on, Brother Branham." Said, "My, look at that crowd," said, "they press about you." And they pulled me out of the crowd. They don't mean to be harsh, but they try to take, 'Cause, friends, sometimes you get around, they tramp you to death almost. And they just pull you. I love them. God bless their hearts.
I'd like to stand right there and touch every one of them and pray for them till I just, and breath will leave out of my body.
Last night when I left the building, they had to lay me out in the car almost. Even my heart and everything, just shaking and jumping, like that.
text_no:
Der sto denne stakkars fargede mannen og ropte: "Å, Pastor."
Og Bror Adams sa, "Kom igjen, Bror Branham." Han sa, "Se på den mengden," og la til, "de trenger seg rundt deg." De dro meg ut av mengden. De mener ikke å være harde, men de prøver å nå fram, for venner, noen ganger blir dere nærmest trampet i hjel. De bare drar i deg. Jeg elsker dem. Gud velsigne deres hjerter.
Jeg skulle gjerne stått rett der og berørt hver eneste en av dem og bedt for dem til jeg bare, til pusten går ut av kroppen min.
I går kveld da jeg forlot bygningen, måtte de nesten legge meg i bilen. Selv hjertet mitt og alt skaket og hoppet, slik.

text: You don't realize the strain of holding them demons when they come out, and they're in the room. And you're responsible. What if something happens in the building? Why, they'd lock you up in jail, and they'd break the meeting for good. Is that, see what you mean? You don't know the responsibility of that. And here then, and so tired anyhow after six months of going.
text_no: Du forstår ikke belastningen ved å holde demoner når de kommer ut, og de er i rommet. Du har ansvaret. Hva om noe skjer i bygningen? De ville arrestere deg og avslutte møtene for godt. Ser du hva jeg mener? Du skjønner ikke hvilken ansvarsfølelse det innebærer. Dessuten er du allerede utslitt etter seks måneders innsats.

text:
Then I--I said, "Wait a minute."
And he was just a crying, poor fellow. He had his hat in his hand, gray headed. He was saying, "Reverend, Reverend."
His wife said, "You's can't get to him, honey. You's can't get to him."
And I said, "Wait a minute. Just a minute." And so I looked around, and I looked at him. I said, "What's the matter, sir?"
And Brother Adams and the other man taken me back up where he was at. And I said, "What's the matter?"
He said, "Reverend, Reverend."
See, he was holding me like that. Said, "Reverend." Putting his hand over on me.
I said, "Yes?"
text_no:
Jeg sa, "Vent litt."
Den stakkars mannen gråt. Han holdt hatten i hånden og var gråhåret. Han sa, "Reverend, Reverend."
Hans kone sa, "Du kan ikke komme til ham, kjære. Du kan ikke komme til ham."
Jeg gjentok, "Vent litt. Bare et øyeblikk." Jeg så rundt meg, og deretter på ham. Jeg spurte, "Hva er problemet, sir?"
Bror Adams og en annen mann førte meg tilbake til der han sto. Jeg spurte igjen, "Hva er problemet?"
Han sa, "Reverend, Reverend."
Han holdt meg slik, og sa: "Reverend." Han la hånden på meg.
Jeg svarte, "Ja?"

text:
He said, "Just a minute, Reverend." He said, "I's come a long ways to come here." He said, "Night before last," said, "my old mammy, she's been dead about twenty years almost." And said, "She was a Christian woman. And I's been blind for better than twelve years." He said, "And last night, or night before last, I dreamed… [Blank.spot.on.tape] That God had sent His gift of Divine healing, and it was at Camden, Arkansas, and for me not to let you get by." Said, "Reverend, please don't pass me by."
You think I could pass him? I don't care who he was. It's God. I don't care how much they'd put you in jail.
text_no:
Han sa: "Et øyeblikk, Pastor." Han sa: "Jeg har reist langt for å komme hit." Han sa: "Natten før forrige natt," sa han, "min gamle mor, som har vært død i nesten tjue år." Og han sa: "Hun var en kristen kvinne. Og jeg har vært blind i over tolv år." Han sa: "Og forrige natt, eller natten før, drømte jeg… [Tomt.område.på.lydbånd] at Gud hadde sendt Sitt gave om guddommelig helbredelse, og det var i Camden, Arkansas, og at jeg ikke skulle la deg gå forbi." Han sa: "Pastor, vær så snill, ikke gå forbi meg."
Tror du jeg kunne gått forbi ham? Jeg bryr meg ikke om hvem han var. Det er Gud. Jeg bryr meg ikke om hvor mye de ville sette deg i fengsel.

text:
Then standing there by that poor old fellow, take a hold of his hands. His eyes had been blowed out by an explosion. Taking a hold of his hands and was praying for him, his wife crying, the tears rolling down off her coat, like that. She's standing there.
After I got through praying, he just--his eyes, he looked so amazed. He'd look around, begin to bat his eyes; he said, "Honey, ain't that an automobile setting right there?" And it was our car that we were setting in. Tears rolling down his eyes, out of his eyes, he begin to raise his hands and rejoice.
Oh, brother, God will testify of His gifts. Heavens and earth might pass, but God's going to move. There's going to be somebody that's going to receive it (Yes, sir.), if you'll come and respect what God has done for you. That's right. God will testify of His gift. It's right now time to get started.
text_no:
Mens jeg sto der ved den gamle mannen, tok jeg hendene hans. Øynene hans hadde blitt ødelagt i en eksplosjon. Jeg holdt hendene hans og ba for ham. Hans kone sto ved siden av, gråtende med tårer som trillet nedover kåpen hennes.
Etter at jeg var ferdig med å be, så han forundret ut. Han begynte å blunke med øynene og sa: "Kjære, er det ikke en bil som står der?" Det var vår bil vi satt i. Tårer trillet fra øynene hans, og han løftet hendene sine og priste Gud.
Å, Bror, Gud vil vitne om Sine gaver. Himmel og jord kan forgå, men Gud vil handle. Noen vil motta det (Ja, det er sant.), hvis du kommer og respekterer det Gud har gjort for deg. Det er riktig. Gud vil vitne om Sin gave. Nå er det på tide å sette i gang.

text:
Listen, not long ago I was in, was over in Illinois. And listen, while we're talking of the colored; this come on my mind. There was… I was in a big coliseum. Many of you know where I'm speaking of. And there looking around and seeing different things, I seen an old colored man with a little ring of white hair around his head; he come along like this, looking. And after while he come to a---a certain thing. He stopped. He took off his hat. He held it behind him. He bowed his head. And I seen he was saying a prayer.
And I walked up to him after he got through praying; I said, "Sir," I said, "I'm a minister." I said, "I seen you praying. What was so startling about the box?"
He looked up at me, his poor old wrinkle hardened cheeks, and tears rolling off the sides of his cheeks. He said, "Rev… Parson," he said, "just look over there."
And I raised up and looked over there, and it looked like a little old dress laying there. I said, "I only see a dress." And that's what many people just see, the natural signs. I said, "I only see the dress."
He said, "You notice that kind of a circle in it, parson?"
And I said, "Yes, sir."
He said, "Feel right here on my side. Said, "There is marks of a slave belt. That's the blood of Abraham Lincoln." Said, "The blood of Abraham Lincoln took the slave belt off of me. Why shouldn't I take my hat off and say a prayer?"
text_no:
Hør her, for ikke lenge siden var jeg i Illinois. Mens vi snakker om de fargede, kom dette på tankene mine. Jeg var i en stor kolosseum. Mange av dere vet sikkert hvilken jeg snakker om. Mens jeg kikket rundt og så forskjellige ting, la jeg merke til en eldre farget mann med en liten ring av hvitt hår rundt hodet; han kom bortover som dette og så seg rundt. Etter en stund kom han til en bestemt gjenstand. Han stoppet, tok av seg hatten, holdt den bak seg og bøyde hodet. Jeg så at han sa en bønn.
Etter at han var ferdig med å be, gikk jeg bort til ham og sa: "Sir, jeg er en forkynner. Jeg så deg be. Hva var det som var så spesielt med denne boksen?"
Han så opp på meg, hans gamle, rynkete, harde kinn, og tårer som trillet ned over sidene på kinnene hans. Han sa, "Pastor," sa han, "bare se der."
Jeg reiste meg og så dit, og det så ut som en gammel kjole som lå der. Jeg sa: "Jeg ser bare en kjole." Og det er hva mange mennesker bare ser, de naturlige tegnene. Jeg sa: "Jeg ser bare kjolen."
Han sa, "Ser du den sirkelformen, pastor?"
Jeg svarte, "Ja, sir."
Han sa, "Føl her på siden min." Så sa han, "Der er merkene etter et slavebelte. Det er blodet til Abraham Lincoln." Han sa, "Blodet til Abraham Lincoln fjernet slavebeltet fra meg. Hvorfor skulle jeg ikke ta av meg hatten og si en bønn?"

text:
And I think tonight, if a colored man, for the freedoms and his right, could take his hat off to the blood of Abraham Lincoln, what ought you Christians to do to the Blood of Jesus Christ. See? Oh, friends, I expected to have more faith in Oakland and L. A. too.
I'll tell you the people that received it not long ago, was the Spanish people at Arizona. They caught the idea right quick. They knew it was God's gift to them. They come right on the platform. And I'd see them dear old Spanish mothers faint and fall and pulled out of line before they could get to me. They believed it.
One of them come, she even had a dream that she… She couldn't even speak English. And she seen that supernatural Being standing. (You got the letter here, have you?) Many… Why? God will testify of His gift. Is that right? Oh, friends, you know what I'm speaking of. Do you understand now what I'm speaking of? Faith, believe it. Come, it isn't the man; it's God in the man. You know what I'm meaning.
text_no:
Og jeg tenker i kveld, hvis en farget mann, for sin frihet og sine rettigheter, kunne ta av seg hatten for Abraham Lincolns blod, hva burde dere kristne gjøre for Jesu Kristi Blod? Ser dere? Å, venner, jeg forventet å finne mer tro i Oakland og L.A. også.
La meg fortelle dere om dem som mottok det for ikke lenge siden; det var de spanske folkene i Arizona. De forstod raskt ideen. De visste at det var Guds gave til dem. De kom rett opp på plattformen. Jeg så de kjære gamle spanske mødrene besvime og bli trukket ut av køen før de kunne nå meg. De trodde.
En av dem kom, hun hadde til og med en drøm om at hun… Hun kunne ikke engang snakke engelsk. Og hun så det overnaturlige Vesenet stå der. (Har du brevet her?) Mange… Hvorfor? Gud vil vitne om Sin gave. Er det riktig? Å, venner, dere vet hva jeg snakker om. Skjønner dere nå hva jeg mener? Tro, tro det. Kom, det er ikke mannen; det er Gud i mannen. Dere vet hva jeg mener.

text:
[End of record]… please, O God, may people come tonight just like they did in the days of the apostles. May they come realizing that this is the apostolic time for them. This is the hour that what they have asked for is now present: the Holy Ghost in the form of healing for their bodies. Grant it, dear God. May many people be healed tonight. Have mercy, Father.
And may everyone that comes tonight, come like they did when they passed by Your humble servant, Simon Peter. And they were laid in his shadow and were made every whit whole, because they recognized God in the man. May they recognize God like they did in Moses. God was in Christ, reconciling the world to Himself. May they recognize God tonight in His people. Grant it, dear Father. Have mercy now and bless every one of them.
And, dear God, at this time I pray for my little church at home that just had services tonight, crying, begging for me to come home. O God, people gathering in from different parts of the country… Take care of my little orphan boy, won't You, dear God.
Oh, I'm out here striving, trying my best, dear God, to obey what I was told to do. And may the people see, Father, what a sacrifice it is, and what You've done for the people. And may they recognize this day and many be healed, and get well of their diseases and testify through this city. For we ask it in the lovely Name of Jesus Christ, Thy beloved Son. Amen.
text_no:
[End of record]… Vær så snill, Gud, la folk komme i kveld slik de gjorde i apostlenes dager. Må de komme og innse at dette er deres apostoliske tid. Dette er timen hvor det de har bedt om, nå er tilstede: Den Hellige Ånd i form av helbredelse for deres kropper. Innvilg det, kjære Gud. Må mange mennesker bli helbredet i kveld. Ha miskunn, Far.
Og må alle som kommer i kveld, komme som de gjorde da de gikk forbi Din ydmyke tjener, Simon Peter. De ble lagt i hans skygge og ble fullstendig helbredet, fordi de gjenkjente Gud i mannen. Må de gjenkjenne Gud som de gjorde i Moses. Gud var i Kristus og forsonte verden med Seg selv. Må de gjenkjenne Gud i kveld i Hans folk. Innvilg det, kjære Far. Ha miskunn nå og velsign dem alle.
Og, kjære Gud, på dette tidspunktet ber jeg for min lille menighet hjemme som nettopp hadde møtene sine i kveld, gråtende, tiggende om at jeg skal komme hjem. O Gud, folk samler seg fra forskjellige deler av landet… Ta vare på min lille foreldreløse gutt, vær så snill, kjære Gud.
Å, jeg er her ute og strever, prøver mitt beste, kjære Gud, for å adlyde det jeg ble fortalt å gjøre. Og må folk se, Far, hvilket offer det er, og hva Du har gjort for folket. Og må de gjenkjenne denne dagen og mange bli helbredet, og bli friske fra sine sykdommer og vitne gjennom denne byen. For vi ber om det i Jesu Kristi, Din elskede Sønns vakre Navn. Amen.
